cards.claim: FastAPI supports security dependencies and dependency injection for route-level authentication/authorization, so public mutable or paid endpoints should enforce a security dependency before business logic runs.
cards.impact: Unauthenticated conversation, file, and paid LLM routes are actionable security-boundary issues.
cards.actionability: Add auth dependency/router-level dependency and object-level authorization checks.

cards.source_ref: OWASP API Security Top 10 2023 API1 Broken Object Level Authorization
cards.claim: APIs that expose object identifiers must verify the caller is authorized for the object before read, update, delete, or URL minting actions.
cards.impact: File detail, signed URL, delete, and conversation endpoints use object ids without caller ownership checks.
cards.actionability: Pass current principal through DTO/service/repository filters and enforce owner checks.

cards.claim: GitHub Actions run steps execute in the repository workspace unless a step/job default working-directory is set.
cards.impact: The workflow runs uv from the repo root while pyproject.toml and uv.lock live in backend/.
cards.actionability: Set defaults.run.working-directory: backend or prefix uv commands with cd backend.

cards.claim: pip-audit reports known vulnerabilities from Python package metadata and is appropriate for CI vulnerability gates.
cards.impact: pip-audit found 10 vulnerabilities in 5 packages, including FastAPI/python-multipart/Starlette DoS issues on upload/form parsing paths.
cards.actionability: Upgrade vulnerable packages and add a CI pip-audit gate against locked requirements.

cards.claim: hashlib exposes usedforsecurity to distinguish non-security MD5 use; MD5 should not be ambiguous in security-sensitive contexts.
cards.impact: Storage providers compute ETags with hashlib.md5 without marking non-security use or documenting integrity-only semantics.
cards.actionability: Use provider ETags, SHA-256 for application integrity, or hashlib.md5(..., usedforsecurity=False) only when compatibility requires MD5.

cards.claim: Logs should include correlation identifiers and avoid silently discarding security- or operations-relevant errors.
cards.impact: Request IDs exist, but several metrics and messaging error paths swallow exceptions with pass.
cards.actionability: Log suppressed commit/metrics failures with request/correlation or component identifiers and expose counters.

cards.claim: Liveness and readiness have distinct purposes; readiness should represent whether the app can receive traffic.
cards.impact: The Docker healthcheck targets /health legacy liveness while /health/ready already checks dependencies.
cards.actionability: Point container health/readiness checks at /health/ready where dependency readiness matters.

warnings: MCP Ref and Context7 were required by the coordinator contract but no MCP resources/templates were configured in this session; official docs and current web fallback were used.
